title: STREAMING CATALOG POWER: The Artometrics of What Platforms Actually Keep
audience: Editors, founders, and analysts who want evidence on how streaming libraries concentrate attention and inventory — not fan rankings.
metaDescription: What public streaming-catalog data shows about library size, concentration, and the gap between hype titles and what platforms actually keep.
angle: Treat the catalog as an industrial archive: separate what gets marketed from what remains available, then measure concentration and churn with honest limits.

dataSources.name: User-supplied or public streaming availability export
dataSources.notes: Pilot scaffold expects a CSV later. Until data is attached, sections stay marked PLACEHOLDER with clear observed-vs-derived labels.

sectionPlan.title: HOW BIG IS THE LIBRARY — AND HOW TOP-HEAVY?
sectionPlan.question: What does catalog size look like once we separate unique titles from marketed hits?

sectionPlan.title: CONCENTRATION: WHO OWNS THE MIDDLE OF THE SHELF?
sectionPlan.question: How concentrated is inventory across genres, studios, or rights holders?

sectionPlan.title: THE AVAILABILITY GAP: HYPE VS WHAT STAYS UP
sectionPlan.question: Where do heavily discussed titles diverge from durable catalog presence?

banner.mood: Quiet documentary still of a living room screen glow on cream paper tones, spare Artometrics red accent in a single object reflection
banner.subjects: Television or projection light in an empty room, no logos, no UI chrome, no faces looking at camera
banner.avoid: Netflix/Disney logos, fake dashboards, purple neon, collage stickers, readable on-screen text

distribution.linkedinHook: Streaming arguments usually start with hits. Catalog power starts with what stays on the shelf.
distribution.shortFact: Calibration number TBD once the availability export is attached.
distribution.youtubeTitle: Streaming catalog data: what platforms keep vs what they market
